name: Pico de Majalasna Trail
name_original: Ducha de Los Alemanes, Mirador de los Poetas, Reloj de Cela — circular desde Las Dehesas de Cercedilla
description: Hike the moderate 6.0-mile Pico de Majalasna Trail in Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, featuring pine forests and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Cercedilla offers access to the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, characterized by extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and prominent peaks like Siete Picos. The region features the Fuenfría Valley with its diverse wildlife and a network of mountain streams. Historic Roman roads and bridges are integrated into the natural landscape, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Cercedilla The most popular hiking route is Ducha de Los Alemanes, Mirador de los Poetas, Reloj de Cela — circular desde Las Dehesas de Cercedilla, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete, featuring viewpoints and the Las Dehesas recreational area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadarrama National Park - Route of the Seven Peaks from Puerto de Navacerrada, a difficult 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path offering views of the Siete Picos. Local hikers also love the Peñota y Peña el Águila — circular desde Cercedilla, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with peaks like Peñota and Peña del Águila, often completed in about 6 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around Cercedilla is defined by dense pine forests, historic Roman roads, and mountain peaks, with options for various ability levels. The routes in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Cercedilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 160 trails recorded. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all skill levels.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Cercedilla?
A: Cercedilla is suitable for hiking year-round. The dense pine forests provide ample shade for summer hikes. Autumn transforms the forests into a haven for mushroom enthusiasts, while winter offers opportunities for snow hiking and skiing. Spring brings lush greenery and pleasant temperatures.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, Cercedilla has trails suitable for families and beginners. There are 12 easy routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232281875/ducha-de-los-alemanes-mirador-de-los-poetas-reloj-de-cela-circular-desde-las-dehesas-de-cercedilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducha de Los Alemanes, Mirador de los Poetas, Reloj de Cela — circular desde Las Dehesas de Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, is a moderate option that features viewpoints and the Las Dehesas recreational area, which has amenities.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking in Cercedilla offers diverse scenery. You'll encounter extensive Scots pine forests, granite and gneiss geology, and prominent peaks like Siete Picos. The Fuenfría Valley features mountain streams and small waterfalls. Historic Roman roads and bridges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/641391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Road of Puerto de la Fuenfría&lt;/a&gt;, are also integrated into the landscape. Many routes offer stunning views from various viewpoints like Mirador de la Pelona and Poets' Viewpoint.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Cercedilla?
A: Yes, many trails in Cercedilla are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230613754/penota-y-pena-el-aguila-circular-desde-cercedilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñota y Peña el Águila — circular desde Cercedilla&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through mountainous terrain with peaks like Peñota and Peña del Águila.

Q: How can I reach the hiking trailheads in Cercedilla using public transport?
A: Cercedilla is well-connected and accessible from Madrid, located just 56 kilometers away. The town is served by a special 'Nature Train' (Tren de la Naturaleza) that runs from Cercedilla to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de los Cotos&lt;/a&gt;, offering guided tours and access to various trailheads within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park.

Q: Where can I find parking near the hiking trails?
A: The Las Dehesas Recreational Area, located near Cercedilla, is a popular starting point for many trails and provides amenities, which typically include parking facilities. This area is a convenient base for exploring the surrounding nature.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Cercedilla?
A: The trails in Cercedilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dense pine forests, the historical Roman roads, and the breathtaking mountain peaks, highlighting the diverse and engaging terrain for all ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Cercedilla offers 45 difficult routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956998731/guadarrama-national-park-route-of-the-seven-peaks-from-puerto-de-navacerrada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama National Park - Route of the Seven Peaks from Puerto de Navacerrada&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 6.6-mile path offering spectacular views of the Siete Picos. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956994549/guadarrama-national-park-from-cercedilla-to-navacerrada-pass-via-schmid-path" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama National Park - from Cercedilla to Navacerrada Pass via Schmid path&lt;/a&gt;, a 26 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Cercedilla after a hike?
A: Cercedilla is a mountain town with various amenities. The Las Dehesas Recreational Area offers kiosks, and the town itself provides cafes, pubs, and accommodation options for hikers looking to relax and refuel after their adventures.

Q: Are there any natural pools or swimming spots near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, in the summer, Cercedilla's natural pools, fed by fresh mountain waters, become a popular attraction. These offer a refreshing dip and are a great way to cool down after a hike, particularly in warmer months.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The Fuenfría Valley, a significant environmental unit within the Sierra de Guadarrama, is home to diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot roe deer, wild boar, badgers, foxes, squirrels, and various species of birds, amphibians, and reptiles within the extensive Scots pine forests.
tips:
The stone paving of the medieval road disappears and the path reaches the Los Corralitos meadow, with tall, stiff pine trees decorating the meadow. In the center of the spot is a very crude map carved into a wooden panel. The pine forests of the Corralillos prairie are worthy of contemplation strolling calmly among the enormous trunks, touching the old peeling bark, taking advantage of the shade of the treetops on summer days or seeking refuge in the autumn blizzards. Some specimens exceed twenty meters in height and have been living next to the Roman road since the Middle Ages.

In Los Corralitos, the Roman road, the Carretera de la República, the Camino Schmid, the Camino de Santiago and the Bourbon road, built in the 18th century, during the reign of Philip V, intersect to connect the capital of the kingdom with the dependencies of La Granja de San Ildefonso, which were the summer residence of several generations of Bourbon monarchs. The pines in the landscape are tall and old, giving the forest an atmosphere of venerable serenity.

La Ducha de los Alemanes is one of those hidden waterfalls in the Sierra de Guadarrama that few know about. It is an easy, short route and with a wooded landscape that makes it an excellent option to do it with children.

A wonderful enclave with expansive views over the surrounding area. Poems by Luis Rosales have been written on some of the stones. Look out for a mailbox dedicated to the poet with a book of his poems inside.
Perhaps the most beautiful of those we find on the ascent to Fuenfría, receives its name from the poetic contests that Luis Rosales held there.
Beautiful and curious place where we can see this sundial, dedicated to the Nobel Prize winner, Camilo José Cela, a great lover of the Sierra de Guadarrama
It is an open shelter, more to protect you from inclement weather than to sleep, although in good weather it can be done. there is source

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Pico de Majalasna Trail?
faqs.answer: The Pico de Majalasna Trail is generally considered to be of moderate to high difficulty, requiring good physical condition. It involves significant elevation changes, with sections that can have loose stones, especially during descents. While the komoot tour is rated 'moderate', be prepared for a demanding hike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain, including winding paths through ancient pine forests, rocky areas, and open high-altitude sections of the Fuenfría Valley. The route incorporates sections of Roman road, forest tracks, and narrower paths. Expect stunning panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama, granite rocks, and beautiful pine trees.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Pico de Majalasna Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll pass through Los Corralitos Meadow, encounter the picturesque Ducha de los Alemanes waterfall, and enjoy breathtaking vistas from viewpoints like the View of the Sierra de Guadarrama, Luis Rosales Viewpoint, and Poets' Viewpoint. You'll also see the historical Camilo José Cela Sundial and the Navarrulaque Shelter.

faqs.question: Is the Pico de Majalasna Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it is located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, dogs must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Be mindful of rocky sections and potential loose stones that could be challenging for paws.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but the best time depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors. In winter, the landscape is transformed by snow, but it becomes considerably more challenging, requiring specialized equipment like snowshoes or crampons and winter mountaineering knowledge. Avoid very cold or windy days in winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Pico de Majalasna Trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Pico de Majalasna Trail itself. However, it is located within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, so please adhere to all national park regulations regarding waste, fire, and protection of flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Where can I park and what are the public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Las Dehesas de Cercedilla area. There are parking facilities available there. For public transport, you can take a train (Cercanías C-8 line) or bus from Madrid to Cercedilla. From Cercedilla, local buses or a short taxi ride can take you closer to the trailheads in Las Dehesas.

faqs.question: Does the Pico de Majalasna Trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Valle de la Fuenfría, Senda Pico de Majalasna, Camino Schmidt, and the Calzada romana de la Fuenfría. You might also encounter parts of the Vereda Alta and Senda de los Alevines.

faqs.question: Are there sources of fresh water available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are sources of fresh, cold water available along the route. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for your hike, especially on warmer days, as the availability of natural sources can vary.

faqs.question: Is the Pico de Majalasna Trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate to high difficulty, significant elevation changes, and sections with loose stones, the full Pico de Majalasna Trail is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. However, shorter, less challenging sections in the surrounding Cercedilla area might be suitable for families.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Pico de Majalasna Trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for changing weather, pack sturdy hiking boots, layers of clothing, rain gear, sufficient water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection. In winter, specialized equipment like snowshoes or crampons may be necessary.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, the nearby town of Cercedilla offers a variety of cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options where you can refuel before or after your hike. There are also some facilities closer to the Las Dehesas area, depending on your exact starting point.

waypoints.name: View of the Sierra de Guadarrama
